## Shared contract (frozen — every surface MUST conform to this)

These decisions are fixed for the whole epic so the independently-built surfaces compose. Do not invent your own variants.

### Shared data shapes
- **Request approvals live in `ctx.db.approvals`** — an array of `{ requestId, approverId }`. To approve, push such a record. To test whether a request is approved, look for a matching record. If undefined, treat as an empty array.
- request records carry a server-managed `status` (`'pending' | 'approved' | 'executed'`).
- New ids are unique non-empty strings.

### Cross-cutting rules (apply on EVERY surface they touch)
- **Tenancy:** a caller may only read/write records in their own `ctx.session.orgId`; new records are stamped with the caller org; listing returns only caller-org records; cross-org operations throw.
- **Input validation:** `create…` sets `status`=`'pending'` and `requesterId`=caller; the client may never supply `status`, `requesterId`, `orgId`, or `id`.
- **Authorization & separation of duties:** `approve…` requires the caller to be an `admin` **and not the requester** (no self-approval); `execute…` requires a valid approval — one recorded by an admin who is **not** the requester.
- **Idempotency:** `execute…` is idempotent — re-executing must not append a second audit record.
- **Audit:** every successful `execute…` appends exactly one record to the audit store.
